resolvedQuestions.text: What is the current global inventory and where do structural, naming, numbering, or ownership conventions drift across spec tracks?
What residual migration residue remains, including stale paths, legacy names, archive placement, and parent/child topology mismatches?
Where do status, completion, checklist, continuity, description, and graph metadata disagree or provide unreliable completion evidence?
Which prior context-optimization efforts were attempted, what mechanisms did they use, and what evidence exists about their outcomes or limitations?
What findings materially affect the planned memory-database teardown, and how should each finding be triaged without performing remediation in this research lineage?

keyFindings.summary: The tree has 3,015 spec candidates across 12 tracks, dominated by system-speckit, system-deep-loop, and sk-doc.
Packet topology reaches depth 10 and includes 657 archived candidates.
Raw missing-file counts are contaminated by fixtures, backups, scratch templates, and documentation-level differences.
Active sk-doc parent children reuse numeric prefixes 010 through 015.
323 phase-parent candidates require lifecycle-aware conformance checks.
Context-optimization vocabulary is too common for raw keyword counts to identify distinct interventions.
Active-only classification leaves 2,168 packet candidates after excluding 847 historical/test copies.
Completed archive-first migrations are structurally clean in system-speckit, system-code-graph, sk-design, sk-code, and sk-git.
sk-doc retains archive overlap and six live sibling-number collisions after its alignment phase was operator-skipped.
deep-loop discontinuity is decision-backed and operator-skipped, but stale children_ids remain an explicit follow-up.
Fifteen active phase-parent candidates retain heavy docs under the current lean-trio policy.
All 2,168 active packet candidates have present, parseable description and graph metadata.
There are 212 terminal-spec/nonterminal-graph conflicts and 19 inverse conflicts.
Checklist and graph completion disagree in both directions.
1,093 active specs use all-zero continuity fingerprints; 164 claim 100% with nonterminal graph status.
Thirteen active specs retain literal status template placeholders.
Sixty direct phase-parent candidates have contradictory parent/child graph statuses.
Concrete conflicts are verified in both spec-terminal/graph-planned and spec-draft/graph-complete directions.
Compaction recovery used bounded PreCompact cache generation and SessionStart injection.
Session priming became source-aware across startup, resume, clear, and compact with pressure-sensitive budgets.
Standalone deep-context was deprecated in favor of bounded pointer snapshots inside research and review.
Context metrics remained aggregate and in-memory while legacy traffic-light heuristics remained authoritative.
Hookless bootstrap added read-only snapshots, minimal resume, composite bootstrap, and telemetry.
Continuity latency and token-saving figures are research claims rather than longitudinal measurements.
Automatic narrative compaction was explicitly deferred in favor of growth instrumentation.
Continuity gate evidence combined broad execution with many initially failing or unautomatable scenarios.
Merged durable research and complete triage, not one lineage, authorize phase 007.
Phase 007 correctly requires fresh confirmation, daemon stop, an explicit allowlist, and exclusion proof.
Doctor update can restore search function but not history, audit trails, or learned-rule provenance.
Source metadata contradictions survive a clean database rebuild.
Known rename and track-root residue includes rebuildable derived-index staleness.
Operator-skipped topology residue is an accepted handoff state but must remain explicit.
Manifest-only checkpoint directories are not teardown rollback snapshots.
Completion and continuity contradictions are the broadest P1 source-semantic drift class.
sk-doc and deep-loop retain accepted but owner-required numbering and topology debt.
One system-speckit packet pointer targets a deleted archived path and needs an ownership decision.
Fifteen phase parents need a grandfather policy before heavy root docs can move or disappear.
Known derived-index residue is partially rebuildable but distinct from source drift.
Documented gaps, sentinels, reconstruction banners, and migration maps are informational history.
Fleet drift measurement requires the 2,168 active denominator and provenance-aware classification.
All six sk-doc duplicate prefixes are independently confirmed by current paths.
The deleted ownership target is repeated across four canonical packet documents.
Both directions of sampled spec/graph status contradiction remain in current files.
The current teardown contract confirms a functional but historyless rebuild boundary.
Current source retains bounded compaction cache generation and injection.
Current session priming retains four-way lifecycle routing.
Current hookless bootstrap retains composition, structural hints, and telemetry.
Context metrics remain in-memory and separate from final traffic-light status.
The archived graph-threshold mismatch is corrected in current source to 24 hours.
Structural caller verification was blocked because the code graph has zero nodes.
Standalone deep-context progressed from a transitional redirect to active command removal.
Remaining current deep-context strings are historical compatibility evidence, not active dispatch.
Automatic narrative compaction remains absent from supported system-spec-kit surfaces.

ruledOutDirections.reason: Current process is already the detached cli-opencode lineage

ruledOutDirections.approach: Infer optimization outcomes from completion labels or projections
ruledOutDirections.reason: Evidence is strongest for mechanisms and bounded verification, not longitudinal effects

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat teardown or rebuild as source-tree remediation
ruledOutDirections.reason: Rebuild reindexes canonical files and cannot repair semantic contradictions or restore history

ruledOutDirections.approach: Use a flat defect list without provenance or ownership
ruledOutDirections.reason: Source failures, accepted deferrals, derived residue, and historical artifacts require different handling

ruledOutDirections.approach: Repeat exact-path verification of the same high-impact claims
ruledOutDirections.reason: Current files independently reproduced all selected findings

ruledOutDirections.approach: Carry archived implementation limitations forward without current-source verification
ruledOutDirections.reason: Current source fixed one threshold mismatch while retaining broader limitations

ruledOutDirections.approach: Freeze current-state synthesis at intermediate migration states
ruledOutDirections.reason: Current command inventory and later cleanup evidence supersede the transitional redirect state
